file_name: AudibleInc_20001113_10-Q_EX-10.32_2599586_EX-10.32_Co-Branding Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Investment Distribution Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: "Law" means any law, ordinance, rule, regulation, order, license, permit and other requirement, now or hereafter in effect, of any governmental authority of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: "Law" means any suggestion, guideline, or recommendation, now or hereafter in effect, issued by any organization.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Law' is changed from legally binding requirements to suggestions from any organization, which contradicts the actual legal definition of 'law'. This will lead to issues of contractual interpretation and enforcement.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification directly contradicts the Administrative Procedure Act (APA), specifically 5 U.S.C. § 551, which defines 'law' in terms of statutes, rules, and orders issued by government agencies with legal authority. The modified definition replaces legally binding governmental mandates with non-binding suggestions from any organization, which fundamentally alters the contract's compliance obligations and enforceability.

Definitions For the purpose of this subchapter- (1) "agency" means each authority of the Government of the United States, whether or not it is within or subject to review by another agency, but does not include- (A) the Congress; (B) the courts of the United States; (C) the governments of the territories or possessions of the United States;

perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Law' replaces legally binding governmental mandates with non-binding suggestions, directly contradicting the Administrative Procedure Act (APA). The original text defines 'Law' as requirements from governmental authorities. The changed text defines 'Law' as suggestions from any organization. This contradicts the legally recognized definition of 'law,' rendering the contract unenforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to 'each authority of the Government of the United States,' which aligns with the original contract's definition, not the altered one.

perturbation.original_text: 11.11 Choice of Law. This Agreement will be interpreted, construed and enforced in accordance with the Laws of the State of Washington, without reference to its choice of Laws rules.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.11 Choice of Law. This Agreement will be interpreted, construed and enforced in accordance with the Laws of the State of Neverland, without reference to its choice of Laws rules.
perturbation.explanation: The Choice of Law provision now specifies "the Laws of the State of Neverland." Since "Neverland" is a fictional place, the contract now has a choice of law provision pointing to a non-existent legal system. This would make it impossible to interpret, construe, or enforce the contract according to its terms.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) - § 1-301. CHOICE OF LAW.
perturbation.law_explanation: The insertion of a fictional jurisdiction contradicts the UCC § 1-301 which requires that the selected jurisdiction have a reasonable relationship to the transaction. Neverland is a fictional location and thus cannot be the governing law of the contract. Furthermore, enforcement of the contract would be impossible since there is no set of codified laws by which to interpret the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-301. Territorial Applicability; Parties' Power to Choose Applicable Law. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-301. Territorial Applicability; Parties' Power to Choose Applicable Law. a) Except as otherwise provided in this section, when a transaction bears a reasonable relation to this state and also to another state or nation the parties may agree that the law either of this state or of such other state or nation shall govern their rights and duties. (b) In the absence of an agreement effective under subsection (a), and except as provided in subsection (c), [the Uniform Commercial Code] applies to transactions bearing an appropriate relation to this state. (c) If one of the following provisions of [the Uniform Commercial Code] specifies the applicable law, that provision governs and a contrary agreement is effective only to the extent permitted by the law so specified: (1) Section 2-402 ; (2) Sections 2A-105 and 2A-106 ; (3) Section 4-102 ; (4) Section 4A-507 ; (5) Section 5-116 ; [(6) Section 6-103 ; (7) Section 8-110 ; (8) Sections 9-301 through 9 - 307 . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text specifies the Laws of the State of Neverland to govern the agreement. The original text specifies the Laws of the State of Washington. Neverland is a fictional location, and thus cannot be a valid jurisdiction. UCC § 1-301(a) states that parties may agree that the law either of this state or of such other state or nation shall govern their rights and duties. A fictional location does not constitute a state or nation. Therefore, the modified text contradicts the UCC § 1-301(a).
